title: TreeDefectCancellation
summary: States an abstract interface that turns the path-wise recurrence into cancellation of slot defects and hence tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: States an abstract interface that turns the path-wise recurrence into cancellation of slot defects and hence tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/TreeDefectCancellation.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A finite linear combination of left-comb tree words of total degree k. Its mathematical data are: support: Finset Comb Index r k; coeff: Comb Coeff Space r k.

The rank-r ambient operator associated to a finite tree-word sum. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, d of type operator data r k, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.operator Sum D.support D.coeff the function sending I to I.to Operator Cal Conf.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} (D : OperatorData r k) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r, k in the natural numbers, D of type Operator Data r k, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, D.to Operator Cal Conf F U equals ∑ I belongs to D.support, D.coeff I times I.to Operator Cal Conf F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,k\colon \mathbb{N},\;D\colon \operatorname{OperatorData} r k,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad D.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U = \sum I \in D.\operatorname{support}, D.\operatorname{coeff} I \cdot I.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r k : ℕ} (D : OperatorData r k) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ I ∈ D.support, D.coeff I * I.toOperator CalConf F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The still-to-be-proved commutator/tangentiality principle for left-comb tree words. Mathematically this is the statement that the path-wise recurrence cancels all slot-wise Q-commutator defects, hence the ambient operator descends to the boundary at the specified input weights. Its mathematical data are: tangential of recurrence: for every D : Operator Data r k, Rank Recurrence the dimension n of the ambient calculus w D.coeff maps to Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights D.to Operator Cal Conf w.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{tangential\_of\_recurrence}&\colon \forall D \colon \operatorname{OperatorData} r k, \operatorname{RankRecurrence} n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} w D.\operatorname{coeff} \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (D.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf}) w\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r k : ℕ) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Apply a supplied defect-cancellation principle to obtain tangentiality. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,k\colon \mathbb{N},\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;H\colon \operatorname{RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality} \operatorname{CalConf} r k w,\;D\colon \operatorname{OperatorData} r k,\;hrec\colon \operatorname{RankRecurrence} n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} w D.\operatorname{coeff},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (D.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf}) w
correspondence.leanType: theorem recurrence_implies_tangential {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (H : RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality CalConf r k w) (D : OperatorData r k) (hrec : RankRecurrence CalConf.n w D.coeff) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) w

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data certifying that a finite tree-word operator is tangential by recurrence. Its mathematical data are: operator Data: Operator Data r k; recurrence: Rank Recurrence the dimension n of the ambient calculus w operator Data.coeff; principle: Recurrence Implies Tangentiality Cal Conf r k w.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{operatorData}&\colon \operatorname{OperatorData} r k\\\operatorname{recurrence}&\colon \operatorname{RankRecurrence} n_{\mathrm{CalConf}} w \operatorname{operatorData}.\operatorname{coeff}\\\operatorname{principle}&\colon \operatorname{RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality} \operatorname{CalConf} r k w\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure TangentialOperatorData (r k : ℕ) (w : Fin r → ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The ambient operator carried by a recurrence-certified data set. In explicit terms, for r, k in the natural numbers, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and d of type tangential operator data cal conf r k w, its defining expression is D.operator Data.to Operator Cal Conf.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (D : TangentialOperatorData CalConf r k w) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The certified tangentiality theorem. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r,k\colon \mathbb{N},\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;D\colon \operatorname{TangentialOperatorData} \operatorname{CalConf} r k w,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} D.\operatorname{toOperator} w
correspondence.leanType: theorem tangential {r k : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (D : TangentialOperatorData CalConf r k w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights D.toOperator w

references.locator: Main ambient construction and tangentiality results
references.note: Motivation for rank-general nested Laplacian words; the Lean tree complex goes beyond what is currently instantiated.

limitations: This file establishes syntax, aggregate imports, or an abstract bridge. It must not be read as a derivation of the corresponding geometric theorem from concrete tensor and analytic data.
